# Design: HTML Mirror Re-conversion Migration

**Status:** Planned
**Date:** 2026-03-15
**Context:** The `text_to_html` converter had several bugs fixed (see `TEXT_TO_HTML_IMPROVEMENTS.md`, issues 1–5). The existing `.utf8.html` files in the Cloudflare R2 bucket (`aozora`) were generated by the old converter and need to be re-converted and re-uploaded where the output has changed.

---

## 1. Scope

All `*.utf8.html` files in R2 bucket `aozora` that differ between the old and new converter output. With ~17,800 books this is a significant batch operation, so only changed files should be uploaded.

The fixes most likely to change output for a given file:

| Fix | Affected files |
|---|---|
| Fix 1 — heading content | Any file with `「...」は大見出し` / `中見出し` / `見出し` commands |
| Fix 2 — unclosed indent blocks | Any file with an unmatched `ここから字下げ` block |
| Fix 3 — `_is_orig()` | Files whose header contains an ASCII line before a role-identified contributor |
| Fix 5 — new inline/block commands | Files using `傍点`, `太字`, `斜体`, `縦中横`, `右に寄せる`, `センタリング`, `割り注` |

---

## 2. Data layout in R2

```
aozora/                       ← R2 bucket
  <book_id>.utf8.txt          ← source text (input)
  <book_id>.utf8.html         ← converted HTML (output to be updated)
  watermark.json              ← import watermark (leave untouched)
```

Both TXT and HTML files are at the bucket root. The custom domain `aozora.ksato9700.com` maps to this bucket.

---

## 3. Migration approach

### 3.1 Why not re-upload all files unconditionally

17,800 unconditional uploads would consume unnecessary R2 write operations and egress, and take longer. Diffing first and uploading only changed files keeps the operation cheap and auditable.

### 3.2 Chosen approach — local TXT + R2 diff + selective upload

TXT files are already available locally at `/Users/ksato/git/py-aozora-data/utf-8/` (17,582 files). There is no need to download them from R2.

```
For each *.utf8.txt in local utf-8/ directory:
  1. Run new converter → new HTML in a temp file
  2. Download existing HTML from R2
     → if not found in R2: skip (intentionally missing books)
  3. Compare: if different → upload new HTML to R2
  4. Log: unchanged / changed / missing(skipped) / error
```

This is a single-pass scan with no intermediate state. If it is interrupted, it can be re-run safely — the comparison step is idempotent.

---

## 4. Implementation

### 4.1 New script: `scripts/migrate_html_r2.py`

A standalone script (not part of the import pipeline) that:

- Reads TXT files from a local directory (default: `/Users/ksato/git/py-aozora-data/utf-8`, overridable via `--txt-dir`)
- Uses the same R2 credentials as `json_backend.py` (`R2_ACCOUNT_ID`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `R2_BUCKET_NAME`)
- For each TXT file, performs the convert → compare → upload loop above
- Supports `--dry-run` (skip uploads, log what would change)
- Supports `--concurrency N` (default 8) for parallel processing with `ThreadPoolExecutor`
- Writes a summary log at the end: total / changed / unchanged / errors

### 4.2 Temp file handling

Use `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile` for the new HTML output. It is deleted automatically after each file is processed.

### 4.3 Comparison

Compute the MD5 of the new HTML in memory and compare it against the **ETag** of the existing R2 object, retrieved via `HeadObject` (headers only — no body download). R2 ETags for single-part uploads are the plain MD5 hex of the content. HTML files are always small enough to be single-part, so this is reliable.

This avoids downloading ~17,582 existing HTML files and makes the comparison step much faster.

### 4.4 Upload content type

Set `ContentType: text/html; charset=utf-8` on all uploaded objects, matching the existing convention.

---

## 5. Prerequisites

1. **Python environment** — `uv sync --extra dev` in `aozora-pages/` (boto3 is already a dependency)
2. **Local TXT files** — available at `/Users/ksato/git/py-aozora-data/utf-8/` (17,582 files)
3. **R2 credentials** — set as environment variables:
   ```
   R2_ACCOUNT_ID=...
   R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=...
   R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
   R2_BUCKET_NAME=aozora
   ```
4. **Converter bug fixes** — branch `fix/text-to-html-bugs` must be checked out (or merged to main)

---

## 6. Running the migration

### Dry run (no uploads — logs what would change)

```bash
uv run python scripts/migrate_html_r2.py --dry-run
```

### Full run

```bash
uv run python scripts/migrate_html_r2.py
```

### Full run with custom TXT directory or more parallelism

```bash
uv run python scripts/migrate_html_r2.py \
  --txt-dir /Users/ksato/git/py-aozora-data/utf-8 \
  --concurrency 16
```

---

## 7. Expected duration and volume

| Step | Estimate |
|---|---|
| List all TXT keys from R2 | < 30 s (paginated) |
| Download TXT + convert + download HTML + compare | ~0.5–1 s per file at concurrency 8 |
| Upload changed HTML | ~0.3 s per file |
| **Total (assuming ~30% of files change)** | **~20–40 min** |

The 30% estimate is conservative; Fix 1 (headings) likely affects the majority of files that have chapter headings. No TXT download step means the bottleneck is R2 reads (existing HTML) and R2 writes (changed HTML only).

---

## 8. Verification

After the migration completes:

1. **Spot-check a known heading file** — open `aozora.ksato9700.com/<book_id>.utf8.html` in a browser and confirm heading text is correct (no raw command string in `<h3>`).
2. **Check the migration log** — review changed / unchanged / error counts.
3. **Validate HTML** — run the W3C validator or a local `html5lib` parse on a sample of uploaded files to confirm no unclosed tags.
4. **Check a file with an English author** — confirm `<h2 class="author">` contains the name, not `<h2 class="original_title">`.

---

## 9. Rollback

The old HTML files will be overwritten in R2. If a rollback is needed, re-run the old converter against the same local TXT files and re-upload. The TXT files are untouched by this migration and serve as the authoritative source.
